stories.title: US-211 integration wiring -- handleCaptureError() into data_logger.py capture loop
stories.intent: US-211 (Sprint 16) shipped BtResilienceMixin.handleCaptureError + classifier (ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE / ECU_SILENT / FATAL) + reconnect loop with backoff schedule + canonical connection_log event types. stories.completionNotes: Wired US-211's handleCaptureError into RealtimeDataLogger._pollCycle via dependency injection. Two new constructor kwargs on RealtimeDataLogger (captureErrorHandler + onFatalError) threaded from lifecycle._initializeDataLogger, which wires them to orchestrator.handleCaptureError + a new lifecycle._onCaptureFatalError that flips shutdownState=FORCE_EXIT with EXIT_CODE_FORCED so systemd Restart=always bounces on FATAL. ECU_SILENT cadence: picked DEFAULT_ECU_SILENT_MULTIPLIER=5 per story allowance ('Ralph picks conservative multiplier'); _getEffectivePollingIntervalMs() multiplies when flag set, _onSuccessfulQuery clears on next good read. Tests: 18 unit (test_capture_loop_integration.py: constructor shape + ADAPTER/ECU/FATAL routing + cadence + cause-unwrap + no-handler backward compat) + 7 integration (test_bt_flap_in_process.py: same PID across flap, canonical connection_log timeline, connection teardown+reopen, FATAL signal, thread identity stable). One pre-existing test updated: test_initializeDataLogger_passesConfig_toFactory now asserts the two new kwargs flow through. Architecture.md Section 5 + docs/testing.md BT walkthrough updated. Fast suite: 3110/3110 passed (3085 baseline + 25 new tests, zero regressions). Scope-fence respected: did NOT touch classifier, backoff schedule, ConnectionRecoveryMixin, drive state machine, or US-199 poll-tier scheduler. Spool's US-211 YELLOW concern from Sprint 16 release-readiness now closed.

stories.title: TD-031 close -- delete dead BatteryMonitor + src/pi/power/battery.py + battery_log table
stories.intent: Close TD-031 by deleting dead code Spool's power audit surfaced. BatteryMonitor (690 LOC in src/pi/power/battery.py) is never instantiated in production (pi.batteryMonitoring.enabled=false in config). Its voltage thresholds (11.0V/11.5V) are 12V automotive or 3S Li values -- this hardware is a 1S LiPo on MAX17048 operating 3.0-4.3V. If an operator ever flips enabled=true expecting battery protection, they get zero coverage. US-216's Power-Down Orchestrator now fully supersedes this class for the shutdown-ladder domain, so the dead code can be removed without functional loss. Deleting reduces audit surface + closes a false-confidence hazard.

stories.title: record_drain_test.py CLI default flip -- --load-class production -> test
stories.intent: Manual invocation of scripts/record_drain_test.py is almost always a CIO-driven scheduled drill (load_class='test'), not a real power-loss event (load_class='production'). The script currently defaults to production, so a CIO who forgets to pass --load-class test during a monthly drill pollutes the production baseline with simulated data. Flip default to test; require explicit --load-class production for the rare case where a human manually records a real drain. One-line change; test update; prevents baseline drift.

stories.intent: Close TD-034 (filed by Ralph Agent 2 at US-216 ship). US-216's PowerDownOrchestrator correctly fires the 30/25/20 SOC stages + legacy suppression + the ladder-vs-legacy regression test, but the four downstream stage behaviors are log-only / best-effort today: WARNING's no_new_drives DB flag doesn't exist; WARNING's SyncClient.forcePush() API doesn't exist; IMMINENT's DriveDetector.forceKeyOff() API doesn't exist (EngineStateMachine has it, DriveDetector delegates); IMMINENT's poll-tier pause hook isn't wired. None of these are bug-fix-critical because the TRIGGER -> systemctl poweroff cascade closes everything via normal service-stop paths, but they're graceful-degradation niceties that give the Pi ~30-45s of additional safety margin during drain events. Spool flagged these as real stage-behavior gaps worth closing.

invariants: AC-restore MUST unwind all stage effects: clear no_new_drives, resume polling, leave collector operational.
no_new_drives does NOT affect currently-active drives -- only gates NEW drive_id minting on cranking transitions.
pausePolling does NOT kill the connection -- poll threads idle but stay attached; resumePolling wakes them.
forcePush is a superset of normal sync, NOT a bypass of correctness -- all normal delta-sync invariants apply.
DriveDetector.forceKeyOff writes a proper drive-end row with a reason field -- downstream analytics see a first-class drive termination, not a gap.
TRIGGER -> poweroff path stays unchanged (US-216 invariant). This story is about WARNING + IMMINENT + AC-restore graceful-degradation.
stopConditions: STOP if adding pausePolling requires significant refactor of the poll-tier dispatcher (thread-unsafe, tightly coupled) -- file inbox note with scope cost; US-225 may split.

STOP if forceKeyOff on DriveDetector conflicts with the existing drive-end detection (30s KEY_OFF debounce from US-200) -- the force path should BYPASS debounce with a reason code, not fight the machine.
STOP if no_new_drives flag persistence requires a new Alembic migration (not just ensure*Table) -- leverage US-213 gate path; do not fork.
STOP if SyncClient.forcePush exposes a state the caller can corrupt (e.g., concurrent-call race) -- add a lock or document the single-writer assumption.
stories.completionNotes: US-225 / TD-034 close -- all 5 log-only stage behaviors wired to concrete actions. New: src/pi/obdii/pi_state.py (singleton no_new_drives gate, ensurePiStateTable + get/set/clear accessors; registered in ObdDatabase.initialize as 'Created pi_state table (US-225)'). DriveDetector.forceKeyOff(reason) + no_new_drives gate on _openDriveId: forceKeyOff acquires the detector lock (existing _endDrive assumes lock held), bypasses RPM/speed debounce, writes a drive_end connection_log row with reason in error_message (new optional param on _logDriveEvent + _endDrive), clears process drive_id, transitions to STOPPED. Safe no-op when STOPPED or STARTING-no-session. Gate check in _openDriveId returns None before nextDriveId() so drive_counter is NOT incremented when gated (invariant verified by test_gateSet_openDriveIdReturnsNoneAndDoesNotIncrementCounter). SyncClient.forcePush() + PushSummary(slots=True) dataclass: wraps pushAllDeltas with explicit-intent logging + aggregate counts (rowsPushed, tablesOk/Empty/Failed/Skipped, disabled flag). No invariants bypassed -- AUTH, retry schedule, failed-push HWM preserve, snapshot skip all inherited. ApplicationOrchestrator.pausePolling/resumePolling: delegates to RealtimeDataLogger.stop/start; connection stays attached (invariant verified by test_pausePolling_doesNotTouchConnection); separate _pollingPausedForPowerDown flag prevents collision with BT-reconnect's _dataLoggerPausedForReconnect; resume no-op when not paused-for-power-down (does NOT steal BT reconnect path); start-raises keeps flag True so AC bounce can retry. Lifecycle stage callback wiring rewrite: onWarning = setNoNewDrives(True) + forceSyncPush(); onImminent = pausePolling + forceKeyOff(power_imminent); onAcRestore = setNoNewDrives(False) + resumePolling. SyncClient built lazily at WARNING via _forceSyncPush helper so a disabled companion service is a benign no-op; broad-exception isolation everywhere so a raising callback never blocks stage escalation (regression test test_warningCallbackRaisesButImminentStillFires + test_imminentCallbackRaisesButTriggerStillFires pin this). BT-close on IMMINENT deliberately NOT wired -- the TRIGGER -> systemctl poweroff cascade handles BT teardown via BtResilienceMixin + systemd service-stop; US-211's reconnect classifier owns the reattach path; US-216 invariant 'TRIGGER -> poweroff path stays unchanged' takes priority. Documented in TD-034 table + architecture.md §10.6 stage-behavior-wiring block + docs/testing.md drill addendum. Scope path corrections during execution: spec said src/pi/data/database_schema.py + src/pi/data/database.py but actual codebase has them under src/pi/obdii/ -- followed actual paths; spec said specs/architecture.md Section 11 but the power orchestrator is in §10.6 (§11 is Deployment) -- wrote to §10.6. 5 new test files (51 new tests, all passing): tests/pi/data/test_pi_state_no_new_drives.py (14: schema/migration/accessors/DriveDetector-gate/reboot-persistence), tests/pi/drive/test_force_key_off_api.py (10: no-op-safety/active-drive-termination/reason-traceability/debounce-bypass/callback-invocation/regression-guard-for-null-error_message-on-normal-drive-end), tests/pi/sync/test_sync_force_push.py (9: summary-aggregation/disabled-companion/AUTH+timeout/failure-isolation+HWM-preserve), tests/pi/obdii/test_poll_tier_pause.py (12: pause/resume idempotency + connection-untouched + raising-logger-swallowed + pause-resume-cycle), tests/pi/power/test_orchestrator_stage_behavior_wiring.py (6: end-to-end 35->TRIGGER with real orchestrator/detector/DB/mock-sync + AC-restore unwind + failing-callback-doesnt-block-escalation). Created tests/pi/drive/ directory + __init__.py (scope specified that path). Verification: targeted suite 51/51 in 78s; ruff clean on all 11 touched files after --fix for import sort; validate_config 4/4 OK; sprint_lint 0 errors, 18 pre-existing sizing warnings (US-225 flags 'sized M but filesToTouch=16 (cap 5); consider L' -- 16 comes from sprint's filesToTouch list which includes the path-corrected originals, accepted at grooming). Scope-fence honored: did NOT touch US-216 state machine, hysteresis, SOC thresholds, battery_health_log schema or BatteryHealthRecorder, ladder-vs-legacy regression test, or US-211 reconnect loop/classifier. TD-034 annotated CLOSED.
